informal: Let $F = \mathbb{Z}_p$ be the field of integers $\mod p$, where $p$ is a prime, and let $q(x) \in F[x]$ be irreducible of degree $n$. Show that $F[x]/(q(x))$ is a field having at exactly $p^n$ elements.
lean:
theorem Herstein_exercise_4_5_16 {p n: ℕ} (hp : Nat.Prime p)
  {q : Polynomial (ZMod p)} (hq : Irreducible q) (hn : q.degree = n) :
  (∃ is_fin : Fintype $ Polynomial (ZMod p) ⧸ span ({q}),
  @card (Polynomial (ZMod p) ⧸ span {q}) is_fin = p ^ n) ∧
  IsField (Polynomial (ZMod p) ⧸ span {q})
reasoning: The Lean signature uses F = ZMod p with p prime, q an irreducible polynomial of degree n, and concludes both that the quotient Polynomial (ZMod p) / span {q} has a Fintype instance with cardinality p^n and that it IsField. The hypothesis q.degree = n correctly pins down the degree (with n coerced into WithBot ℕ). The ideal span {q} is the principal ideal (q), so the quotient is F[x]/(q(x)). Both claimed conclusions — being a field and having exactly p^n elements — match the informal statement, with no extra or missing hypotheses.
